1.0.0.en: Initial release: Electricity, Gas and Export meter devices with automatic discovery, half-hourly prices, standing charge, price level, last-24h usage and cost/earnings, Homey Energy integration, account balance, smart-charge planner, carbon intensity, tariff comparison, reporting, and a rich set of dynamic-pricing, saving-session, dispatch and balance Flow cards.

1.0.1.en: Initial public release: electricity, gas and export meters with automatic discovery, half-hourly Agile pricing, standing charges, Homey Energy integration, account balance, smart-charge planner, carbon intensity, a rich set of dynamic-pricing Flow cards, and an Agile Prices widget with a Today/Tomorrow toggle that highlights the current half-hour and the cheapest slots.

1.0.2.en: Adds an Agile Prices widget with a Today/Tomorrow toggle, a half-hourly chart that highlights the current slot, and a ranked “best times to use power” list (cheapest first, configurable). Clearer live-power setting and reliability improvements.

1.0.3.en: Fixed Energy integration so the electricity, export and gas meters now appear in Homey Energy as whole-home cumulative meters.

1.0.4.en: Renamed the app to Octopus Energy and refreshed the meter driver images with pink illustrations on white backgrounds for App Store certification.

1.0.5.en: Improves the Agile Prices widget refresh behavior so it reloads when reopened and recovers from stale overnight price data.

1.0.6.en: Clarifies the Agile Prices widget chart timeline by showing the full day through 24:00.

1.0.7.en: Fix a rare price-feed stall: add request timeouts and roll the Agile price reliably at every half-hour so price triggers fire on time.

1.0.8.en: Improves Agile and Timeline widgets for negative prices, hardens refresh and month-cost calculations, clears dependency audit findings, strengthens CI/publish checks, adds a gas price-below trigger, and improves Economy 7 tariff comparison accuracy.

1.0.9.en: Improved meter capability icons and reorganised electricity, gas, and export device capability layouts.

1.0.10.en: Improved price and charging automations, refresh reliability, multi-account support, security, API efficiency, and billing accuracy.

1.0.11.en: Adds optional PayPal donation support through Homey's native Donate button.

1.0.12.en: Improves App Store discovery and directs support to the Octopus Energy Homey Community topic.

1.0.13.en: Fixes electricity meters going offline when current tariff rates are omitted, updates Octoplus points and Saving Sessions/Power Up integrations, and adds Saving Sessions diagnostics.

1.0.14.en: Improves meter Repair and pairing reliability, adds privacy-safe integration diagnostics and data freshness indicators, strengthens tariff comparison and account caching, and improves widget accessibility.

1.0.15.en: Fixes electricity meters that showed a connection error when only the current tariff price was briefly unavailable — the device now stays online with a clear advisory instead. Adds automatic recovery for a mismatched stored tariff, stops repeated Octoplus points errors on accounts without loyalty points, and adds privacy-safe price diagnostics to speed up future troubleshooting.

1.0.16.en: Adds guarded current-price recovery for Intelligent Octopus Go accounts using legacy or newer four-rate tariff contracts, while keeping household and EV-specific prices distinct.

1.0.17.en: Reduces Kraken API pressure with shared, budgeted live-power polling; improves Intelligent Octopus Go dispatch handling with device-aware SMART, BOOST and cancellation states; and clarifies Power Up Flow names as Free Electricity (Power Up).

1.0.18.en: Fixes Intelligent Octopus Go electricity meters that could show a blank price and a connection warning: the household day/night rate now recovers from your Octopus account even after a tariff-code change, and expected API rate-limit skips no longer appear as errors.

1.0.19.en: Reliability & efficiency fixes: Saving Sessions and Free Electricity now keep refreshing after the first check; the Intelligent dispatch status in Settings shows correctly again; the 'dispatching now' indicator is more accurate and no longer sticks after a failed check; and startup makes fewer redundant Octopus API calls to stay well within the account rate limit.
